rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when they refer to ports with visible names and valid types.
Use AGREE 'assume' statements for input bounds when the requirement specifies concrete numeric ranges.
Generate AGREE constants for named thresholds and use them in boolean expressions for range checks.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid and use only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Assumptions and guarantees should be scoped to the appropriate component type or implementation level.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data types of the referenced features.
Do not invent bounds or constraints not explicitly stated in the requirement.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use `const` for named constants and `eq` for boolean expressions in contracts
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments or guarantees in component type scopes
Do not assume numeric types for Boolean or event ports without explicit model support
Do not introduce AADL property names as AGREE variables without model exposure
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
